summary: F-LOW DK1 was already landed driver-side: PoolEngine now elects exactly one final drop with an AtomicUsize handle count, and its regression test releases 16 clones through a Barrier before asserting one physical close.

verification: Commit review: Clone increments handles; Drop uses fetch_sub(Ordering::AcqRel) and only prev == 1 calls close_all_connections.

Commit review: racing_pool_handle_drops_close_exactly_once creates 16 clones, releases them through std::sync::Barrier, joins every drop thread, waits for worker exit, and asserts backend.closed == 1.

A fresh CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/lavender-dk1 cargo test -p oracledb --lib racing_pool_handle_drops_close_exactly_once was bounded to 180 seconds and timed out while compiling asupersync; no current-session passing test result is claimed.
